codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal implementation that conditionally applies a wider max-width (1920px) CSS class to the shell root when on the dashboard route using useLocation(), and changes the detail grid from 1fr 1fr to 1fr 2fr so the request log table gets ~2/3 of available width. Only 2 files changed (App.tsx, styles.css) with 10 insertions and 3 deletions. CSS specificity and cascade order are correct. The existing 980px responsive breakpoint already overrides .dashboard-detail-grid-wide to 1fr, so stacked mobile layout is preserved. Other pages (Chat, Credentials, Tools) are unaffected since the class is route-conditional.

issues.description: The catch-all route (path="*") renders <DashboardPage /> but isDashboard only matches "/" and "". Users navigating to unknown routes (e.g., /nonexistent) will see the dashboard content at the narrower 1400px layout instead of 1920px. Minor inconsistency — not user-facing in practice since there are no links to invalid routes, and the visual difference is cosmetic.
The check `location.pathname === ""` is defensive but unnecessary — React Router always normalizes pathname to at least "/". Harmless, but could be removed for clarity.

summary: Pass. The implementation correctly widens the dashboard layout for wide displays (max-width bumped from 1400px to 1920px, dashboard-specific) and gives the request log table more space (1fr 2fr grid). The approach is minimal and non-disruptive: only 2 files touched, CSS specificity is correct, responsive breakpoints are preserved, and non-dashboard pages are unaffected. Worker followed the skill procedure correctly, performed thorough visual verification at 4 viewport sizes plus 2 non-dashboard pages, and all automated checks (typecheck, tests) passed. Two non-blocking nits noted: catch-all route doesn't get the wider layout, and the empty-string pathname check is redundant.
